Method provenance and key results
Property: Atom-condensed Fukui reactivity indices (f+, f–, f0, dual descriptor) for acrolein; headline figure reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui function value f+ = 0.64232102 on atom 4 (Cβ of the C=C–C=O chain).
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI (density fitting) as implemented in PySCF (version not explicitly reported by engine).
Geometry provenance: single-point calculations performed on the user-supplied Cartesian coordinates in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/acrolein.xyz; no further optimisation (DFT//input-xyz).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvation).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (0, 1) for the N electron state; cation (+1, 2) and anion (–1, 2) states were computed for f±.
Software / backend: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill using PySCF for all three single-point DFT calculations; calculation launched via
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 …
SCF convergence: all three SCF calculations converged; no non-convergence flags reported.
